name: Eiffel Tower
clue: This iron lattice tower was built as the entrance arch for the 1889 World's Fair and was originally intended to be demolished after 20 years.
funFact: It was the world's tallest man-made structure for 41 years.

name: Machu Picchu
clue: This 15th-century Inca citadel sits at 2,430 metres above sea level in the Andes mountains, often called the 'Lost City of the Incas'.
funFact: It was unknown to the outside world until 1911 when Hiram Bingham III visited the site.

name: Great Wall of China
clue: This ancient series of walls and fortifications stretches thousands of miles across northern East Asia, built over many centuries to protect against invasions.
funFact: It is not actually visible from space with the naked eye, contrary to popular belief.

name: Colosseum
clue: This elliptical amphitheater in the heart of a European capital could hold between 50,000 and 80,000 spectators and hosted gladiatorial contests.
funFact: Its construction began under Emperor Vespasian around 70-72 AD.

name: Taj Mahal
clue: This ivory-white marble mausoleum was commissioned by a Mughal emperor in the 17th century as a tomb for his favorite wife.
funFact: Over 20,000 artisans worked on its construction, which took about 22 years.

name: Sydney Opera House
clue: This multi-venue performing arts centre is one of the most distinctive buildings of the 20th century, with its distinctive shell-like roof structures on a harbor.
funFact: Danish architect Jørn Utzon won the design competition in 1957 with his revolutionary design.

name: Amazon River
clue: This massive South American river carries more water than the next seven largest rivers combined, flowing through the world's largest tropical rainforest.
funFact: During the wet season, the river can be up to 30 miles wide.

name: Mount Everest
clue: At 8,849 metres, this is Earth's highest mountain above sea level, located in the Himalayas on the border between two Asian countries.
funFact: Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay made the first confirmed ascent on May 29, 1953.

name: Grand Canyon
clue: This steep-sided canyon carved by a river in the American Southwest is 277 miles long, up to 18 miles wide, and over a mile deep.
funFact: The canyon exposes nearly two billion years of Earth's geological history.

name: Niagara Falls
clue: This group of three waterfalls on the border between the United States and Canada is the most powerful waterfall in North America.
funFact: More than 3,160 tons of water flows over the falls every second.

name: Victoria Falls
clue: Known locally as 'The Smoke That Thunders', this waterfall on the border between two southern African countries is considered the world's largest.
funFact: The spray from the falls can be seen from up to 50 km away.

name: Sahara Desert
clue: This is the world's largest hot desert, spanning most of North Africa. Despite its barren image, only about 25% of it is covered in sand.
funFact: Approximately 4,000–10,000 years ago, the Sahara was much greener and wetter.

name: Tokyo
clue: This is the world's most populous metropolitan area, a megacity known for its neon-lit skyscrapers, historic temples, and cutting-edge technology.
funFact: It was formerly known as Edo and became the imperial capital in 1869.

name: Cairo
clue: This sprawling African city is the largest in its continent and sits at the gateway to ancient wonders, including giant stone structures built over 4,500 years ago.
funFact: The city's name derives from the Arabic Al-Qāhirah, meaning 'The Triumphant'.

name: Rio de Janeiro
clue: This South American city is famous for its iconic Christ the Redeemer statue, Carnival festival, and stunning natural harbor.
funFact: It was the capital of Brazil until 1960 when Brasília was inaugurated.

name: New York City
clue: The most populous city in the United States, known as 'The Big Apple', home to a famous green copper statue gifted by France in 1886.
funFact: Over 800 languages are spoken in this city, making it the most linguistically diverse urban area in the world.

name: London
clue: This European capital on the Thames River has been a major settlement for over two millennia and is home to a famous clock tower and a red double-decker bus culture.
funFact: It became the world's first city to reach a population of 1 million people, around 1811.

name: Dubai
clue: This ultramodern city in the Arabian Peninsula rose from a small fishing village to a global metropolis in just decades, home to the world's tallest building.
funFact: Over 90% of its residents are expatriates from around the world.

name: Moscow
clue: This city is home to a famous square flanked by a colorful cathedral with onion-shaped domes, and serves as the capital of the world's largest country by area.
funFact: The Moscow Metro system is famous for its ornate, palatial stations built in the Soviet era.

name: Beijing
clue: This East Asian capital contains the world's largest palace complex, a 'Forbidden City' that served as the home of emperors for nearly 500 years.
funFact: It has served as the national capital for more than 800 years.

name: Cape Town
clue: This South African city sits at the southwestern tip of the continent, dominated by a famous flat-topped mountain that is often shrouded in clouds called a 'tablecloth'.
funFact: It is one of the most biodiverse cities in the world, home to a unique floral kingdom.

name: Buenos Aires
clue: This South American capital is known as the 'Paris of South America' and is the birthplace of the tango, a passionate dance and music style.
funFact: It has the widest avenue in the world, Avenida 9 de Julio, spanning 16 lanes.

name: Istanbul
clue: This Turkish city is the only major city in the world that spans two continents, and was once the capital of three empires including the Ottoman Empire.
funFact: It was previously known as Constantinople and before that as Byzantium.

name: Nairobi
clue: This East African capital is unique in having a national park within its city limits where lions and giraffes roam against a backdrop of skyscrapers.
funFact: Its name comes from a Maasai phrase meaning 'cool waters' or 'place of cool waters'.

name: Mumbai
clue: This Indian megacity is the financial capital of the country and home to Bollywood, the world's most prolific film industry by number of films produced.
funFact: It was previously known as Bombay until 1995.

name: Angkor Wat
clue: This 12th-century temple complex in Southeast Asia is the world's largest religious monument, originally built as a Hindu temple before converting to Buddhism.
funFact: It appears on the national flag of Cambodia.

name: Petra
clue: Known as the 'Rose City' due to the color of the stone from which it is carved, this ancient Middle Eastern city was the capital of the Nabataean Kingdom.
funFact: It was featured in Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade as the location of the Holy Grail.

name: Stonehenge
clue: This prehistoric monument on a British plain consists of a ring of standing stones weighing up to 25 tonnes, built over 4,000 years ago for unknown purposes.
funFact: The larger stones were transported from a quarry about 25 miles away.

name: Chichen Itza
clue: This pre-Columbian city built by the Maya civilization in Mexico features a famous step pyramid that casts a serpent-shaped shadow during the equinoxes.
funFact: The pyramid has exactly 365 steps, one for each day of the year.

name: Acropolis of Athens
clue: This ancient citadel perched on a rocky outcrop above a Mediterranean capital contains the remains of several ancient buildings of great architectural and historic significance.
funFact: The Parthenon was used as a temple, treasury, church, mosque, and arms depot over its history.

name: Mount Fuji
clue: This symmetrical stratovolcano is the highest peak in an island nation and has been a sacred site and source of artistic inspiration for centuries.
funFact: It last erupted in 1707 and is considered one of the world's most iconic mountains.

name: Tromsø
clue: This Norwegian city above the Arctic Circle is one of the best places on Earth to see the Northern Lights dancing in the night sky.
funFact: It is nicknamed the 'Gateway to the Arctic' and experiences the midnight sun in summer.

name: Galapagos Islands
clue: This remote volcanic archipelago in the Pacific Ocean inspired Charles Darwin's theory of natural selection after his visit in 1835.
funFact: Giant tortoises here can live over 170 years and were the primary inspiration for the islands' name.

name: Great Barrier Reef
clue: This is the world's largest coral reef system, stretching over 2,300 km off the northeastern coast of Australia, visible from outer space.
funFact: It comprises over 2,900 individual reefs and 900 islands.

name: Lake Titicaca
clue: This is the world's highest navigable lake, straddling the border between two South American countries at an elevation of 3,812 metres.
funFact: Floating islands made entirely of totora reeds are inhabited by the Uros people on this lake.

name: Dead Sea
clue: This salt lake bordered by Jordan and Israel is Earth's lowest point on land at 430 metres below sea level, with salinity so high that swimmers naturally float.
funFact: Nothing can live in its waters due to its extreme salinity, which is about ten times saltier than the ocean.

name: McMurdo Station
clue: This is the largest research station on the southernmost continent, serving as the main hub for scientific exploration in Earth's most extreme environment.
funFact: The surrounding region has not received rain in two million years, making it Earth's driest desert.

name: Reykjavik
clue: This is the northernmost capital city of a sovereign state, located in a volcanic island nation famous for geysers, hot springs, and stunning natural landscapes.
funFact: Almost all of its electricity comes from renewable geothermal and hydropower sources.

name: Timbuktu
clue: This fabled city in the Sahara Desert of West Africa was once one of the most important centers of Islamic scholarship and trans-Saharan trade in the 15th century.
funFact: The city's name has become synonymous in many cultures with a remote, faraway place.

name: Zanzibar
clue: This semi-autonomous archipelago off the coast of East Africa is famous for its spice trade history, white-sand beaches, and historic Stone Town.
funFact: Freddie Mercury, lead singer of Queen, was born here in 1946.

name: Havana
clue: This Caribbean island capital is famous for its well-preserved colonial architecture, vintage American cars, and vibrant salsa music scene.
funFact: It is the largest city in the Caribbean and was founded by the Spanish in 1519.

name: Kathmandu
clue: This Himalayan capital sits in a valley surrounded by mountains and serves as the gateway for expeditions to the world's highest peaks.
funFact: The valley contains seven UNESCO World Heritage Sites within close proximity.

name: Marrakech
clue: This North African city's ancient medina is a maze of souks, mosques, and palaces, known for its main square that fills nightly with entertainers and food stalls.
funFact: It is nicknamed the 'Red City' due to the distinctive rose-red sandstone walls and buildings.

name: Venice
clue: This Italian city is built on 118 small islands separated by canals, where gondolas replace cars and the city slowly sinks about 1-2mm per year.
funFact: The city was built on millions of wooden piles driven into the lagoon floor.

name: Kyoto
clue: This Japanese city served as the imperial capital for over a millennium and is famous for its thousands of classical Buddhist temples, gardens, and geisha districts.
funFact: It was removed from the US atomic bomb target list due to its historical and cultural significance.

name: Uluru
clue: This massive sandstone monolith in the heart of the Australian outback is sacred to the indigenous Anangu people and changes color at sunrise and sunset.
funFact: About two-thirds of the rock lies underground; it is estimated to extend 2.5 km into the earth.

name: Cape of Good Hope
clue: This rocky headland at the southern tip of the African continent was historically significant as the point where sailors could round Africa's southern extremity.
funFact: Despite popular belief, it is not the southernmost point of Africa — Cape Agulhas is.

name: Mount Kilimanjaro
clue: This dormant volcano in East Africa is the highest peak on the African continent and the world's tallest free-standing mountain.
funFact: It has three volcanic cones: Kibo, Mawenzi, and Shira.

name: Torres del Paine
clue: This national park in southern South America is home to dramatic granite towers, turquoise lakes, and glaciers at the southern tip of the Andes mountain range.
funFact: The park sits on a remote peninsula and its name refers to the three distinctive granite spires at its heart.

name: Bora Bora
clue: This French Polynesian island is surrounded by a turquoise lagoon and a barrier reef, known worldwide as a luxury honeymoon destination in the South Pacific.
funFact: Its name in the local language means 'first born', and its peak is the remnant of a dormant volcano.

name: Gobi Desert
clue: This large cold desert spanning parts of China and Mongolia was an important part of the ancient Silk Road and is known for rich dinosaur fossil discoveries.
funFact: It is one of the few deserts to experience snowfall, due to its high altitude.

name: Palace of Versailles
clue: This opulent French royal palace was the seat of political power during the reign of Louis XIV and was the site of the treaty that ended World War I.
funFact: The Hall of Mirrors contains 357 mirrors arranged in 17 arched panels.
